engine: Cloudbeds 'myfrontdesk' booking engine — modern Chakra-UI build (React). Served from hotels.cloudbeds.com.

propertyIds.Sea Esta Komodo: UNKNOWN — homepage behind SiteGround sgcaptcha interstitial; not reached headlessly

deepLinkFormat: checkin + checkout (ISO date). Confirmed working on Saltline (Cy0z7M) and Fatwave (SoRbvN). Dates populate the Check-in/Check-out buttons AND auto-fire the search, so room cards render immediately — NO calendar interaction required.
deepLinkNotes: ?date=...&nights=N does NOT work (UI stays empty, Search button disabled). The working pair is checkin + checkout. &adults=2 is accepted by the URL but the guest stepper still renders '1 Guest' (the param is read for availability pricing but not reflected in the stepper UI) — non-blocking, search returns rooms regardless.

iframe.notes: Cloudbeds offers both an embeddable widget iframe AND a full-page redirect. For the friction test, BYPASS the homepage entirely and goto the canonical reservation URL directly (deep-link). Saltline embeds a widget (a#cb-widget-submit 'CHECK AVAILABILITY' on the homepage). Fatwave 'Book Now' links out to a full-page reservation URL.
iframe.recommendation: Do NOT drive the homepage iframe. Use the deep-link directly — faster, deterministic, avoids per-site Book-affordance selector variance.

cookieDismiss.notes: Only relevant on the hotel homepage (Saltline). Not present on the cloudbeds.com engine page. Irrelevant when deep-linking.

captcha.notes: The Cloudbeds reservation engine shows NO visible CAPTCHA, NO Cloudflare Turnstile, NO reCAPTCHA widget, NO challenge cookie (verified headlessly). navigator.webdriver=false under playwright-extra+stealth. Individual hotel homepages may sit behind a host-level bot-wall: Sea Esta Komodo returns a SiteGround sgcaptcha interstitial (.well-known/sgcaptcha/) that blocks the homepage headlessly — another reason to deep-link straight to hotels.cloudbeds.com.

steps.desc: Load the date-prefilled reservation URL directly (bypass homepage). Dates auto-fill + search auto-runs.
steps.waitFor: button.cb-select-button (the per-rate 'Add' button) — proves room results rendered

steps.desc: Wait for the first rate-plan 'Add' button to be visible (room availability has loaded). Typically 5-7s after goto under networkidle.
Optional sanity check: the Check-in button's aria-label reads 'Check-in, <Mon DD, YYYY>' (proves the deep-link dates applied).
steps.altSelector: the two buttons with class 'chakra-button d-1nt62lc' whose text is a formatted date

steps.desc: Click the first available rate's 'Add' button to add the room to the cart. aria-label is 'Add <Rate Name>' (e.g. 'Add Standard Rate').
steps.blockReason: Click is delivered to a visible, enabled button but the React onClick handler does NOT fire under Playwright — confirmed across 6 strategies (plain click, force, real mouse down/up, focus+Enter, native PointerEvent+MouseEvent dispatch, parent-row click). Zero network requests fire on click (delta 0). Cart stays 'No Accommodations Added'. No CAPTCHA/Turnstile present. Consistent with an isTrusted / trusted-event guard in Cloudbeds' Chakra cart code that silently drops synthetic events. This is an AGENT-BLOCK, not a selector problem.
steps.cartSelector: .cb-shopping-cart (text 'No Accommodations Added' = empty; populated shows room name + price + a checkout button)
steps.impact: Cannot proceed past add-to-cart. Steps 5-7 are the documented human-flow selectors, observed but NOT reachable headlessly.

steps.selector: TBD — cart checkout button (text expected to be 'Reserve Now' / 'Checkout' / 'Continue'); only renders after a room is successfully added
steps.desc: Documented next step after a successful Add: the .cb-shopping-cart sidebar populates and reveals a checkout CTA. Selector not confirmable headlessly because step 4 is blocked.

steps.selector: Guest-details form fields (First name / Last name / Email / Phone) — standard Chakra inputs
steps.desc: Cloudbeds collects guest details before payment. Specific input names not captured (blocked upstream).
Payment page indicators to look for: URL segment /payment OR /confirm; a card-number iframe (Stripe Elements / Cloudbeds hosted card field); input[autocomplete='cc-number']. NOT reached headlessly.

paymentReachedReason: Blocked at add-to-cart (step 4). The Cloudbeds Chakra booking engine silently drops synthetic 'Add' clicks — the React onClick never fires (0 network requests, cart stays empty) despite a visible+enabled button and no CAPTCHA/Turnstile. This is an isTrusted-style trusted-event guard — a deliberate agent-resistance measure. Calendar, search, and room-listing all work headlessly; only cart mutation is blocked.
gotchas: DEEP-LINK IS THE WAY IN: https://hotels.cloudbeds.com/reservation/<ID>?checkin=YYYY-MM-DD&checkout=YYYY-MM-DD&adults=2 — pre-fills dates, auto-runs search, renders room cards. Skip the homepage entirely. (The older ?date=&nights= format does NOT work on the Chakra engine.)
Date format in the URL is strict ISO: YYYY-MM-DD (e.g. 2026-08-16).
&adults=2 in the URL prices availability for 2 pax but the on-page guest stepper still shows '1 Guest' (cosmetic mismatch, non-blocking).

Calendar day cells (if ever needed) are div[data-date='YYYY-MM-DD'].chakra-stack.day with aria-disabled='true' on blocked days; month nav has no aria-label match for 'next' (use a button whose aria-label/text matches /^next|>|›|→/).
Room-selection button text is 'Add' (NOT 'Book Now'/'Reserve'/'Select'). Selector: button.cb-select-button, aria-label 'Add <Rate Name>'.

AGENT-BLOCK at add-to-cart: 6 click strategies all fail to mutate the cart. Treat Cloudbeds Chakra as NOT automatable past room-selection for the friction test — record steps 1-4 as the reachable frontier and mark the payment step as agent-blocked.

body.innerText on the engine page includes the JSON-LD <script> schema (LodgingBusiness) as the first ~600 chars — when scraping visible text, filter to specific tags (h1-h4, p, button, label) or strip <script> first.

Per-site Book affordance on the homepage varies wildly: Saltline = a#cb-widget-submit 'CHECK AVAILABILITY' (Cloudbeds widget); Fatwave = 'Book Now' link. This is WHY deep-linking is strongly preferred over a homepage-driven flow.

Sea Esta Komodo homepage is gated by a SiteGround sgcaptcha interstitial (.well-known/sgcaptcha/) — headless cannot pass it. Reservation property ID not recovered. To include Sea Esta, find its cloudbeds reservation ID another way (page source via a residential/headed fetch, or the hotel's booking link) then deep-link.
Currency is auto-appended by the engine (?currency=usd for Saltline, ?currency=php for Fatwave) based on geo/IP — harmless, do not set it manually.

testDates.rule: T+45d / T+47d (Protocol v2 §4 rolling Study-B dates)
